task_completion.description: Navigate to the CAMEO Chemicals website
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open and displaying the CAMEO Chemicals website (cameochemicals.noaa.gov).

task_completion.description: Research the physical properties of the four specified chemicals
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent searching for or viewing the chemical data pages for Acetone, Toluene, Methanol, and Ethyl Acetate on the CAMEO Chemicals website.

task_completion.description: Create the vapor hazard report file in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows a file named 'vapor_hazard_report.txt' being created or saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: List the Vapor Pressure and Boiling Point (with units) for each chemical in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'vapor_hazard_report.txt' include the Vapor Pressure and Boiling Point values, along with their respective units, for Acetone, Toluene, Methanol, and Ethyl Acetate.

task_completion.description: Identify the chemical with the highest vapor pressure and greatest inhalation hazard
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report explicitly states that Acetone has the highest vapor pressure and poses the greatest inhalation hazard among the four chemicals.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the CAMEO Chemicals website to gather data
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the CAMEO Chemicals interface to find the properties, rather than relying on external search engines or hallucinated knowledge.

integrity.description: The agent created the report file legitimately
integrity.visual_evidence: The report is seen being edited in a text editor or created via standard terminal commands, rather than just echoing a pre-written string without research.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Acetone has the highest vapor pressure and is the greatest inhalation hazard. Approximate Boiling Points: Acetone (56.1°C), Methanol (64.6°C), Ethyl Acetate (77°C), Toluene (110.6°C). Approximate Vapor Pressures at 20°C: Acetone (181-187.5 mmHg), Methanol (94-97 mmHg), Ethyl Acetate (73 mmHg), Toluene (21-28 mmHg). Values may vary slightly based on the specific data source within CAMEO Chemicals, but Acetone must be identified as having the highest vapor pressure.
